What Lingers? - The New York Times","content":"Season 4 of the Emmy-winning FX series “The Bear” ended last summer with an episode called “Goodbye,” in which the Chicago fine-dining restaurant the Bear exhausted the last of the capital fronted by its primary investor, Jimmy Kalinowski (Oliver Platt)."},{"url":"https://www.esquire.com/entertainment/tv/a71745553/the-bear-season-5-finale-ending/","title":"The Bear Season 5 Finale Ending Explained","content":"Alongside Sydney (Ayo Edebiri), Richie (Ebon Moss-Bachrach), Marcus (Lionel Boyce), Tina (Liza Colón-Zayas), and everyone who works in the restaurant, Carmen decides to help them through one final night of service.
In the finale, Carmen receives a call from Michelin inspector Peter Clark, who visited the restaurant in season 4 when Richie created artificial snow for the patrons.
As the finale eventually reveals, he asks his cousin Stevie (John Mulaney) to help set him up for an interview with an architectural firm."},{"url":"https://mashable.com/entertainment/the-bear-season-5-ending-what-happened-to-carmy","title":"'The Bear' ending explained: What happens to Carmy? | Mashable","content":"The Bear earned not one but two Michelin stars from Michelin inspector Peter Clark (Gary Janetti), who visited the restaurant in Season 4.
Outside the kitchen, Richie (Ebon Moss-Bachrach) gets the opportunity to fly to Japan for a hospitality conference, and Jess (Sarah Ramos) is coming with him.
Elsewhere, Ebra (Edwin Lee Gibson) kickstarts the Beef's ghost kitchen empire, with emphatic approval from Carmy (Jeremy Allen White).
Between a blowup fight over his quitting announcement to his panic over a dropped lamb dish, Season 5 made it clearer than ever to Carmy that working in a restaurant simply isn't healthy for him.
Episodes also air Thursday nights at 9 p.m."},{"url":"https://decider.com/2026/06/25/the-bear-season-5-ending-explained/","title":"‘The Bear’ Season 5 Ending Explained: Do The Chefs Earn A Michelin Star? Does The Restaurant Close? And What Happens To Carmy? | Decider","content":"The question is, it also closing time for The Bear, the family-owned restaurant that our favorite fictional chefs desperately tried to save in the fifth and final season, which hit Hulu on Thursday, June 25.
With no time left in their deal with Uncle Jimmy (Oliver Platt) and “The Computer” (Brian Koppelman), limited food, and depleted finances, the chefs came together to carry out a final service in hopes of earning a miracle Michelin star.
A couple who got engaged at The Bear work at Pellegrino and invited Richie to an international hospitality seminar in Japan.
Beyond the restaurant’s walls Marcus says goodbye to Luca at the airport, Tina’s husband celebrates her new CDC job, the boys scout franchise locations, and Syd spends a day off with her proud dad."},{"url":"https://www.npr.org/2026/06/25/nx-s1-5866448/the-bear-season-5-review","title":"Review: 'The Bear' Season 5 returns to the restaurant : NPR","content":"Sydney (Ayo Edebiri) and Carmy (Jeremy Allen White).
What you see is the core story of The Bear, which is people trying to serve food and overcome problems, but through the lens of everything that has happened over the show's run: Carmy's retreat from his obsessiveness, Richie's expansive (and inspiring) discovery of his gift for hospitality; Sydney's stepping forward from second-in-command to leader; Tina's complex relationship with the restaurant and her grief over Mikey; Sugar and Carmy's relationship with Donna (Jamie Lee Curtis); the arrival of Marcus as a high-end pastry chef."},{"url":"https://www.rogerebert.com/streaming/the-bear-season-5-fx-tv-review-2026","title":""The Bear" Closes Its Doors With a Trimphant Final Service ...","content":"Jun 25, 2026 — “The Bear” Closes Its Doors With a Trimphant Final Service in Season 5. {"query":"The Bear season 5 final episodes synopsis restaurant relationships theme","follow_up_questions":null,"answer":null,"images":[],"results":[{"url":"https://www.esquire.com/entertainment/tv/a71745553/the-bear-season-5-finale-ending","title":"The Bear Season 5 Finale Ending Explained","content":"The series ends on a family gathering for Richie's daughter's birthday, which is the the last moment we spend in the restaurant. After winning","score":0.74961954,"raw_content":null},{"url":"https://deadline.com/gallery/the-bear-series-finale-recap-ending-explained","title":"'The Bear' Series Finale: How Do Things End For Carmy ...","content":"# ‘The Bear’ Is Over: Here’s What Happens To Carmy, Sydney, Richie & More In Series Finale. All eight episodes of the fifth and final season dropped on Thursday evening, concluding the series run by picking up the morning after the events of the Season 4 finale in which Sydney (Ayo Edebiri), Sugar (Abby Elliott) and Richie (Ebon Moss-Bachrach) find out that Carmy (Jeremy Allen White) is leaving the restaurant. It also just so happened to be the night that The Bear’s 30-day timer, representing the vestiges of Uncle Jimmy’s (Oliver Platt) money to keep the lights on, ran out. A majority of the season takes place over the course of one day, as the kitchen prepares for and executes one final service in hopes of earning its first Michelin star, because they believe the man who may bestow it upon them will be dining there that night. Carmy, Richie, Sydney and Sugar decide not to tell the rest of the staff about Carmy’s departure, but the tension gets to the group and Sydney eventually drops the news in a moment of frustration.","score":0.71377134,"raw_content":null},{"url":"https://www.indiewire.com/criticism/shows/the-bear-season-5-episode-8-review-finale-explained-1235202502","title":"'The Bear' Season 5 Episode 8 Review: The Series Finale, Explained","content":"# ‘The Bear’ Finale Is All About the Memories. 'The Bear' Season 5 Episode 8 stars Jeremy Allen White as Carmy. *Editor’s note: The following review contains **spoilers** for “[The Bear” Season 5, Episode 8, “The Original Beef of Chicagoland” — the series finale. In the third episode of the fourth season of “The Bear,” The Bear won not one, but two Michelin stars. If you didn’t realize, I wouldn’t blame you — Carmy (Jeremy Allen White) and Syd (Ayo Edebiri) sure didn’t know Peter Clark (Gary Janetti) was there the night Richie (Ebon Moss-Bachrach) treated a vacationing family to a snowy summer night in Chicago. (His big stoop talk with Claire ends the episode.) Timing his arrival so early in the season also helps to push him out of mind, all of which is key if we’re going to buy into the tension of the penultimate episode’s dinner service, where the whole team has to come through, again and again, in order to ensure Mr. Dearborn has the perfect meal.","score":0.69399214,"raw_content":null},{"url":"https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=L8ucZKlcMrM","title":"THE BEAR Season 5 Ending Explained","content":"THE BEAR Season 5 Ending Explained

I review, breakdown and explain The Bear Season 5. I discuss the final season of the FX show which stars Jeremy Allen White, Ayo Edebri, Jon Bernthal, Jamie Lee Curtis and Ebon Moss-Bachrach. I react to the ending where Carmy walked away from the restaurant, Richie and Jess going to Japan, Sydney getting two Michelin stars on the restaurant, Sugar repairing her relationship with Donna, the influence of Mikey on the process and the franchise of The Beef. I analyze the deeper meaning of family in this series and how it contributed towards every character such as Tina, Marcus, Luca, Gary, Ebra, Pete and Jimmy's endings.
